text.title: Change Control Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the change-control methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Every change MUST have a written Change Request before work begins; verbal agreement is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: CR MUST include impact analysis across scope, schedule, cost, quality, and risk before CCB review.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: On CR approval, baseline MUST be updated and re-published; un-updated baselines cause drift.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: CR decisions (approved/rejected/deferred) MUST be logged with date, decider, rationale — never edited.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: CCB decisions MUST meet quorum (≥3 voting members including Sponsor delegate) — single-person approvals invalid.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip for agile-only delivery without baselines, or single-dev experiments.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
